artifacts.summary: Root cause: extension loader overwrites active commands with outdated extension source, stripping COMMAND EXECUTION MODE preamble and routing fixes

memory_candidates.content: OpenCode extension reload (via Neovim extension picker) unconditionally overwrites active command files (.opencode/commands/*.md) with extension source files (.opencode/extensions/core/commands/*.md). Any improvements made directly to active command files are lost on reload. The fix is to keep the extension source in sync with the active files by backporting improvements. The loader.lua copy_file() function has no version checking or drift detection.

The COMMAND EXECUTION MODE preamble in OpenCode commands prevents the LLM from describing commands instead of executing them. All 15 core commands in ~/.config/nvim/.opencode/extensions/core/commands/ are missing this preamble (only in active ~/.config/nvim/.opencode/commands/ files). Extension reload strips the preamble, causing silent describe-instead-of-execute failures.

~/.config/nvim/.opencode/output/ is OpenCode session export directory (created when user presses <leader>x = session_export keybind). Files named after last command invoked. If OpenCode runs with cwd=~/.config/nvim/ rather than intended project directory, session exports and artifact writes target wrong specs/ directory.

active_projects.description: Fix the "Restore last session" and "Browse all sessions" options in the OpenCode session picker (<C-CR> -> OpenCode) based on the root cause identified in task 575.
Diagnose the actual current failure modes for the OpenCode session picker invoked via <C-CR> -> OpenCode. Both restore and browse options fail despite a prior fix attempt (task 544).

artifacts.summary: Diagnosed OpenCode session picker failure modes: duplicate terminal creation race, missing port config, redundant toggle() call, and fire-and-forget select_session API
Implementation plan with 4 phases to fix duplicate terminal creation race in OpenCode session picker
Implementation summary for task 575: fixed OpenCode session picker duplicate terminal race, made start() idempotent, and configured explicit port 3000

active_projects.completion_summary: Fixed OpenCode session picker failure modes by removing redundant toggle() from restore/browse paths, making start() idempotent via snacks.terminal.get() guard, and configuring explicit port 3000 to bypass slow pgrep/lsof discovery. All 4 phases completed. Files modified: ai-tool-picker.lua and opencode.lua.

Implementation plan to fix bare mktemp calls in update-recommended-order.sh and setup-lean-mcp.sh across all 4 directory copies
Implementation summary: replaced all bare mktemp calls with mktemp -p specs/tmp tmp.XXXXXXXXXX across 8 files (4 copies of update-recommended-order.sh + 4 copies of setup-lean-mcp.sh)

Root cause: OpenCode Glob tool silently returns 0 results for relative paths starting with .opencode/ (hidden directory). ProofChecker and 5 other child projects have unfixed routing. 9 issues cataloged.
4-phase plan to fix OpenCode extension routing across 6 child projects, create sync script, and add validation warnings
Fixed OpenCode extension routing across 5 child projects, created sync script, added routing validation warnings

memory_candidates.content: OpenCode Glob tool limitation: relative paths starting with .opencode/ (hidden directory prefix) return 0 results. Absolute paths work correctly. Fix: use project_root=$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel) then glob $manifest_dir/*/manifest.json.

OpenCode child project commands (implement.md, research.md, plan.md) are NOT automatically updated when the nvim config versions are fixed. Core system commands in .opencode/commands/ are set up once during project initialization and never automatically synced.

OpenCode Glob tool silently returns zero results for relative paths starting with hidden directories. All manifest discovery code must use absolute paths derived via git rev-parse --show-toplevel. Core routing commands are not managed by the extension loader and require manual propagation to child projects.

OpenCode Glob tool silently returns zero results for relative paths into hidden directories. Fix: derive project root via git rev-parse --show-toplevel then use $project_root/.opencode/extensions for manifest discovery. Use sync-core-commands.sh to propagate to child projects.

When multiple projects share common command infrastructure, use a sync script pattern: one canonical source (nvim .opencode/commands/) and a registry of child project paths. The script supports --dry-run, --check (detect drift), and --commands (select subset). Template: .opencode/scripts/sync-core-commands.sh.

active_projects.completion_summary: Fixed OpenCode extension routing failure (task 129 lean routing bug) by replacing relative-path Glob-based manifest discovery with absolute git-root-derived paths in all 5 child project command files, created a reusable sync script (sync-core-commands.sh) for future propagation, and added explicit routing validation warnings to the canonical nvim source for implement.md, research.md, and plan.md.

artifacts.summary: Research report on using Discord to manage OpenCode agents from Neovim, covering existing infrastructure, Neovim integration plugins, and everyday workflow
Implementation plan with 2 phases for creating a Discord+OpenCode+Neovim workflow guide

memory_candidates.content: The discord-bot Neovim integration requires DISCORD_BOT_LINK_TOKEN to be set to any non-empty string in the shell environment, even though the bot side has LINK_API_TOKEN empty (no auth enforced). The Neovim plugin (discord-link.lua) returns an error at line 52 when TOKEN is nil/empty, before making any HTTP request. Set DISCORD_BOT_LINK_TOKEN to any value in home.sessionVariables to enable the integration.

The discord-bot.service has a recurring heartbeat blocking issue: nextcord.gateway warnings indicate the asyncio event loop is stuck, making the aiohttp HTTP API server (port 8080) unresponsive. Fix: systemctl restart discord-bot. The HTTP API is needed for Neovim leader>ar (link) and leader>as (session picker) commands to work.
The OpenCode serve endpoint is fixed at port 4096 (127.0.0.1:4096). The NixOS service config uses --port 4096 explicitly. The bot service has OPENCODE_SERVER_URL=http://127.0.0.1:4096 hardcoded.

active_projects.description: Investigate and implement context: fork + agent: frontmatter for core delegating skills to enable CLAUDE_CODE_FORK_SUBAGENT=1 cache sharing, following corrected understanding of fork mechanics from official Claude Code docs

artifacts.summary: Revised plan to investigate and implement context: fork + agent: frontmatter for core delegating skills, enabling FORK_SUBAGENT cache sharing with 4 phases: audit, pilot, rollout, documentation

Definitive architecture analysis: hybrid inline-agent approach has no precedent, context: fork + orchestration IS proven (slide skills), cache sharing is fundamentally incompatible with named agent routing, recommend rescoping task 500 to documentation-only

memory_candidates.content: All loaded extension skills (neovim-research, neovim-implementation, nix-research, nix-implementation) use the same Pattern A delegation as core skills -- explicit Task tool with subagent_type and multi-stage postflight. They do NOT use context: fork or agent: frontmatter. Only unloaded extension skills (e.g., slide-planning, slide-critic) use Pattern B with context: fork.

The present extension's skill-slide-planning and skill-slide-critic skills use context: fork + agent: in frontmatter AND explicit subagent_type in their Task call. This proves that context: fork with multi-stage orchestration (preflight, interactive Q&A, delegation, postflight) works correctly.
Claude Code agent definitions support a skills field that preloads skill content into the subagent context at startup. This is described as the inverse of context: fork.

active_projects.description: Optimize team-mode skills (team-research, team-plan, team-implement) to maximize FORK_SUBAGENT parallel cache sharing benefits for teammates 2-N

artifacts.summary: Team-mode skills are already eligible for FORK_SUBAGENT cache sharing; optimization focuses on prompt restructuring, dynamic token multiplier calculation, and cache-aware metadata reporting.
4-phase plan to restructure team-mode prompts for FORK_SUBAGENT cache sharing, replace hardcoded token multiplier with dynamic formula, and update documentation

memory_candidates.content: TeammateTool spawns in team-mode skills do NOT specify subagent_type, making them eligible for CLAUDE_CODE_FORK_SUBAGENT=1 cache sharing. Teammates 2-N inherit the parent's prompt cache prefix, reducing input token cost by ~90%. Both CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS=1 and CLAUDE_CODE_FORK_SUBAGENT=1 must be enabled. The actual cache benefit depends on the byte-identical shared prefix ratio -- prompts should put shared content first and teammate-specific assignments last to maximize overlap.

skill-team-research hardcodes team_size=4 in Stage 1 regardless of user-provided --team-size flag. The four fixed roles are: Primary (A), Alternatives (B), Critic (C), Horizons (D). The token_usage_multiplier is hardcoded at 5.0 in all team skill metadata but should be dynamically calculated.
